scores.correctness_notes: The response correctly covers writing to app databag, checking is_leader(), using custom events, and setter methods. However, it only covers the charmhub-hosted (legacy) approach via `charmcraft fetch-lib` and misses the current PyPI packages approach (`charmlibs.interfaces.*` namespace) which the gold standard explicitly highlights as the primary modern option.
scores.hallucination_notes: none — all illustrative class/method names are clearly presented as generic patterns, and the underlying ops framework APIs used (ops.Object, ops.RelationEvent, ops.ObjectEvents, ops.EventSource, relation.data[app].update) are real and correct
